contract.name: v2/download/contract.md
contract.description: Creates a download job for a specific contract award and returns job metadata plus the backend configuration snapshot for the generated archive.

award_id.description: Identifier of the target contract award. Accepts canonical contract award strings (e.g., 'CONT_AWD_…') and the internal numeric award identifier.
award_id.constraints: required; rejects null or empty strings; value must resolve to an existing contract award; purely numeric strings are accepted and resolve to the associated award.

file_format.description: Optional override for the archive format applied to all files in the download.
file_format.constraints: optional; defaults to 'csv'; accepts only lowercase values in ['csv','tsv','pstxt']; other strings return HTTP 400 with an explicit enum message; null or numeric JSON values currently trigger an HTML 500 response.

status_url.description: Absolute HTTPS endpoint for polling the download job status; includes file_name as a query parameter.

file_url.description: Absolute HTTPS URL where the generated archive is published once the job finishes.

download_request.description: Snapshot of the backend download configuration applied to the job.

account_level.description: Aggregation level used for generated datasets; observed value 'treasury_account'.

award_id.description: Internal numeric award identifier associated with the request.

download_types.description: Dataset identifiers included in the archive; observed to always contain the contract trio regardless of request input order.

file_format.description: Format applied to generated files, echoing the requested or default format.

filters.description: Filters applied to the download; includes the internal award_id and contract award type codes.

award_id.description: Internal award identifier used for filtering.

award_type_codes.description: Set of contract award type codes automatically applied (A, B, C, D).

include_data_dictionary.description: Indicates whether the archive bundles the contract data dictionary.

include_file_description.description: Supplemental readme packaged with the archive.

destination.description: Filename used for the supplemental readme inside the archive.

source.description: Backend source path for the supplemental file.

is_for_assistance.description: Flag indicating the job targets assistance data (observed false).

is_for_contract.description: Flag confirming the job targets contract data (observed true).

is_for_idv.description: Flag indicating whether the job targets an IDV award (observed false).

limit.description: Row ceiling applied to generated datasets; observed 500000.

piid.description: Contract PIID associated with the award.

request_type.description: Descriptor for the job request type; observed value 'contract'.

quirks: Response file_name pattern is CONT_<PIID>_<timestamp>.zip without the _transactions/_awards suffix documented.
API accepts both numeric award IDs and numeric-string award IDs even though the docs only describe string values.
Additional body keys such as download_types are ignored; the backend always expands to the default contract dataset trio.
status_url and file_url are returned as absolute HTTPS links rather than the relative examples shown in the markdown docs.
The production IPv6 listener often closes connections without a response; forcing IPv4 delivers the JSON payloads reliably.
Supplying null or numeric values for file_format currently yields an HTML 500 error instead of JSON validation feedback.
risks: Download jobs are asynchronous; clients must poll status_url until status='finished' before using file_url.
Clients that prefer IPv6 may see empty replies because the IPv6 listener drops connections; applications should retry over IPv4.
Non-string file_format inputs surface server-side 500 errors, so callers should validate the enum client-side.
Generated archives can be large (limit 500000 rows), which may impact download time and storage.

probes.notes: pass - minimal request succeeds and defaults file_format to csv.
pass - numeric-string award_id resolves to the same contract metadata when routed via IPv4.
pass - invalid file_format rejected with explicit enum list.
fail - null file_format triggers an HTML 500 response.
pass - status endpoint confirms completion for the generated file.

mismatches: Documentation claims file_name ends with a timestamp plus _transactions/_awards, but production responses use CONT_<PIID>_<timestamp>.zip instead.
risks: Download jobs are asynchronous; clients must poll status_url until status='finished' before using file_url.
The IPv6 listener drops connections, so callers should retry over IPv4 when they receive empty replies.
Non-string file_format inputs currently raise 500 HTML responses, requiring client-side validation.
Generated archives can be large (limit 500000 rows), potentially impacting download size and processing time.
